data: Museum epigraphicum: seu inscriptionum Christianarum quae in Syracusanis catacumbis repertae sunt corpusculum

V. Strazzulla, Museum epigraphicum: seu inscriptionum Christianarum quae in Syracusanis catacumbis repertae sunt corpusculum, Documenti per servire alla storia di Sicilia : 3. ser. 3 (Palermo: Lo Statuto, 1897)
Antonio Ferrua, «Epigrafica sicula pagana e cristiana», Rivista di Archeologia Cristiana 18 (1941): 151–243
Five lines, with left and right justification, vacat below.
Lunate epsilon and sigma, mu tending to cursive.
Strazzulla, 1897, Museum epigraphicum: seu inscriptionum Christianarum quae in Syracusanis catacumbis repertae sunt corpusculum
Ferrua, 1941, Epigrafica sicula pagana e cristiana

Four lines, centred on the stone, diminishing in size from top to bottom.

Funerary inscription for Eirene and Theodoulos
hederae at the four corners, and two christograms to the right of the final line in a circle, with a cross between them and below.

funerary inscription for eirene and theodoulos
over five lines, consistent left margin.
traces of red paint noted in the inventory

Five lines of Greek letters following the left margin of the stone.
Somewhat irregular lunate letters, tending strongly to cursive, especially the eta, alpha, mu and xsi
later Roman, but not certain whether Christian or Pagan (Ferrua)

Orsi describes the writing as very irregular semi-cursive, scratched in the first two lines, formless ('informi') in the others.
Lunate sigma (quadrate) and broken bar alpha
Rough and superficial letters ("rozze e superficiali lettre").
Described by Orsi as poor quality lettering, with rubrication.
Described by Orsi as 'a buone lettere'.
